headline: General Motors (NYSE:GM) Exceeds Q1 CY2026 Expectations
summary: Automotive manufacturer General Motors (NYSE:GM) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, but sales were flat year on year at $43.62 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $3.70 per share was 41.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: General Motors (NYSE:GM) Exceeds Q1 CY2026 Expectations
content.summary: Automotive manufacturer General Motors (NYSE:GM) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, but sales were flat year on year at $43.62 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $3.70 per share was 41.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: GM releases 2026 first-quarter results, declares quarterly dividend
summary: General Motors (NYSE: GM) today reported first-quarter 2026 revenue of $43.6 billion, net income attributable to stockholders of $2.6 billion, and EBIT-adjusted of $4.3 billion.

content.title: GM releases 2026 first-quarter results, declares quarterly dividend
content.summary: General Motors (NYSE: GM) today reported first-quarter 2026 revenue of $43.6 billion, net income attributable to stockholders of $2.6 billion, and EBIT-adjusted of $4.3 billion.

headline: GM earnings: US auto market still 'resilient' coming off tariffs, oil shocks
summary: Automaker General Motors (GM) reported mixed first quarter results on Tuesday, posting $43.62 billion in quarterly revenue (just missing estimates) alongside adjusted earnings of $3.70 per share (above and beyond expectations of $2.62). TD Cowen equity analyst Itay Michaeli looks back on GM's past quarter as the company navigated improving tariff conditions, elevated oil (CL=F, BZ=F) and gas prices (RB=F), and the opportunities that are still there in EV markets. Also, be sure to catch Yahoo Finance's interview with General Motors CFO Paul Jacobsen.

content.title: GM earnings: US auto market still 'resilient' coming off tariffs, oil shocks
content.description:
Automaker General Motors (GM) reported mixed first quarter results on Tuesday, posting $43.62 billion in quarterly revenue (just missing estimates) alongside adjusted earnings of $3.70 per share (above and beyond expectations of $2.62).

TD Cowen equity analyst Itay Michaeli looks back on GM's past quarter as the company navigated improving tariff conditions, elevated oil (CL=F, BZ=F) and gas prices (RB=F), and the opportunities that are still there in EV markets.

Also, be sure to catch Yahoo Finance's interview with General Motors CFO Paul Jacobsen.
content.summary: Automaker General Motors (GM) reported mixed first quarter results on Tuesday, posting $43.62 billion in quarterly revenue (just missing estimates) alongside adjusted earnings of $3.70 per share (above and beyond expectations of $2.62). TD Cowen equity analyst Itay Michaeli looks back on GM's past quarter as the company navigated improving tariff conditions, elevated oil (CL=F, BZ=F) and gas prices (RB=F), and the opportunities that are still there in EV markets. Also, be sure to catch Yahoo Finance's interview with General Motors CFO Paul Jacobsen.

headline: GM expects a $500 million tariff refund from Trump levies the Supreme Court struck down
summary: General Motors is expecting a $500 million tariff refund after the Supreme Court struck down some of President Donald Trump's most sweeping levies.  On Tuesday, GM said it's now looking to rake in $13.5 billion to $15.5 billion in earnings before interest and taxes this year — up from previous forecasts of $13 billion to $15 billion.  The refund is set to ease the company's total tariff expenses some.

content.title: GM expects a $500 million tariff refund from Trump levies the Supreme Court struck down
content.summary: General Motors is expecting a $500 million tariff refund after the Supreme Court struck down some of President Donald Trump's most sweeping levies.  On Tuesday, GM said it's now looking to rake in $13.5 billion to $15.5 billion in earnings before interest and taxes this year — up from previous forecasts of $13 billion to $15 billion.  The refund is set to ease the company's total tariff expenses some.
